title: Imperfect competition
description: Practice what you have learned about the difference between perfect competition and different forms of imperfect competition in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
Petunia’s Pears sells pear cider. There is only one other seller in the market.

**What kind of industry is the market for pear cider?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The market for walrus hats has one seller and many buyers. Assume all walrus hats sold are identical.

**What kind of industry is the market for walrus hats?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**All of the following are imperfectly competitive markets EXCEPT:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following describes a monopsony market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a few buyers, a few sellers

assessment_items.question_content:
Priya’s Party Supplies sells cake toppers in a market with many other sellers, but each seller has their own version of cake toppers. 

**Which of the following statements is true based on this information?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Priya’s marginal revenue curve is less than her demand curve.
Priya produces a socially optimal quantity.
Priya produces in a perfectly competitive industry.
Priya can charge whatever price she wants to for her cake toppers.
Priya’s average total cost curve is below her average variable cost curve.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is true about an imperfectly competitive firm’s marginal revenue (MR) curve if it has a linear and downward-sloping demand curve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: MR decreases and is less than demand.
MR decreases at an increasing rate.
MR increases at first, then decreases.

assessment_items.question_content:
**If a firm must lower its price to sell more of a good, which of the following must also be true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Its marginal revenue curve is lower than its demand curve.
Its marginal revenue curve is equal to its demand curve.
Its marginal revenue curve is higher than its average total cost curve.
Its marginal revenue curve is the same as its marginal cost curve.
Its marginal revenue curve is upward-sloping.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is true about production in an imperfectly competitive market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Less is produced than is socially optimal.
More is produced than is socially optimal.
The amount produced minimizes average total cost.
The amount produced minimizes marginal cost.
The amount produced is the same as in perfect competition.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In an imperfectly competitive market, which of the following is true about the quantity produced?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**For the graph shown here, what quantity will this firm produce and what price will it charge?**
 

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The firm chooses the quantity where marginal cost equals marginal revenue.
[[☃ image 1]]

The firm charges the price that buyers are willing to pay for that quantity.
[[☃ image 1]]

The firm will produce $Q_1$ and charge a price of $P_4$

options.alt: A graph illustrates the relationship between cost, price, and quantity in a market model. The Y-axis represents cost and price in dollars, while the X-axis represents quantity. The following lines and curves are displayed: A straight downward-sloping demand line, labeled "D." A straight downward-sloping marginal revenue line, labeled "M R." An upward-sloping marginal cost curve, labeled "M C." A U-shaped average total cost curve, labeled "A T C." A U-shaped average variable cost curve, labeled "A V C." Key intersections are shown: The "M C" curve intersects the "A T C" curve at its minimum point. The "M C" curve intersects the "A V C" curve at its minimum point. The "M C" curve intersects the "M R" line at quantity Q 1, corresponding to price P 1. The "D" line intersects the "A T C" curve at price P 3 and quantity Q 2. Dashed lines extend horizontally from price levels P 4, P 3, P 2, and P 1, and vertically from quantities Q 1 and Q 2.

assessment_items.question_content:
The market for space travel has the possibility to be very profitable, but it is also likely to be imperfectly competitive. 

**Which of the following is the most likely reason that the space travel industry is imperfectly competitive?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: low minimum efficient scale relative to the size of the market

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following would most likely occur in a market with high start-up costs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: fewer firms and prices higher than marginal cost
many firms and prices lower than marginal revenue
few firms with a price equal to marginal cost
few firms and price lower than marginal revenue

assessment_items.question_content:
**All of the following are barriers to entry EXCEPT:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: exclusive ownership of a key resource
